name: Abderrahim Amlou
summary: Researcher and software engineer focused on quantum networking systems, embedded software, experimental control infrastructure, and network automation.
about: I design software and measurement infrastructure for complex systems where research-grade experimentation needs reliable engineering. My work spans quantum communication platforms, distributed measurement software, programmable network backends, secure embedded systems, and simulation-driven evaluation of next-generation networks.

highlights.label: Professional roles across research, cybersecurity, IoT, and AI
Joined NIST to work on quantum networking systems
Ongoing research in computer science and quantum networks
Languages spoken across technical and international environments

currentFocus: Real-time software agents for quantum measurement and control
Simulation and AI-based evaluation of quantum network performance

experience.title: Embedded Software Engineer and Quantum Networks Researcher
experience.subtitle: National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST), Gaithersburg, Maryland, USA
points: Built real-time Python control software for quantum measurement systems, including coincidence detection, source control, and White Rabbit time synchronization.
Designed a distributed measurement plane to orchestrate experiments and collect data across multiple quantum network nodes.
Implemented network automation backends for programmable optical switches using NETCONF, RESTCONF, and OpenConfig.
Extended quantum network simulation workflows with custom entanglement swapping logic and evaluation models.

experience.title: Cybersecurity Engineer
experience.subtitle: Schneider Electric, Angouleme, France
points: Integrated Infineon Trust-M secure elements with ARM-based industrial microcontrollers for device authentication and secure communications.
Implemented secure boot, firmware signing, and TLS communication use cases for embedded industrial systems.
Developed embedded C firmware for cryptographic operations using hardware security modules.

experience.title: IoT Engineer
experience.subtitle: KYNEX Digital Business, Phnom Penh, Cambodia
points: Built an end-to-end IoT safety system for motorcycles with embedded sensing, Bluetooth communication, and an Android application.
Programmed Arduino-based controllers to collect and transmit real-time data on speed, tilt, and vibration.

experience.title: AI Developer
experience.subtitle: Soft Center, Rabat, Morocco
points: Built Python-based machine learning models to improve driver-passenger matching in a ride-sharing application.
Prepared user data and integrated recommendation logic into the application backend.

education.title: Ph.D. in Computer Science - Quantum Networks
education.subtitle: University of Grenoble Alpes, Grenoble, France
points: Research topic: Towards an AI-based framework for quantum network evaluation.
Focus on simulation, performance modeling, entanglement generation, and machine-learning-based optimization.

education.title: Master's Degree in Software for Embedded Systems
education.subtitle: University of Western Brittany, Brest, France
points: Specialized in design, simulation, and verification of secure embedded systems.
Master's thesis with Schneider Electric on secure hardware modules for encrypted communications and firmware updates.

education.title: Engineering Degree in Embedded Systems
education.subtitle: National Institute of Posts and Telecommunications (INPT), Rabat, Morocco
points: Five-year engineering program covering electronics, embedded software, FPGA development, systems design, and networking.

leadership.title: Vice Chair
leadership.subtitle: IEEE Communications Society, Washington DC Section
points: Coordinated technical events focused on communications and quantum networking technologies.

leadership.title: General Secretary
leadership.subtitle: IEEE Engineering in Medicine and Biology Society, Washington DC Section
points: Supported logistics and communication for health-tech and AI events.

leadership.title: Humanitarian Organizer
leadership.subtitle: CAS Charity Club, INPT, Rabat, Morocco
points: Helped organize humanitarian aid missions supporting families in remote High Atlas regions.
